title: Level 620
wikitext:
__NOTOC__ {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 620: "Salt Caverns"}} {{Background|file=thesaltmine.png}} {{Headerbox|file=coalandsalt.jpg|header='''The salt awaits you'''}} {{Alt Class|no=620|ent=2|ext=3|env=3}} Level 620 is the 621st level of the Backrooms.
Description
{{Blurimage|file=thesaltmine.png|caption=An early photo of Level 620}} '''Level 620''', colloquially known as "Salt Mines," is a large and damp salt mine that contains of millions of salt crystals and coal rocks that are spread around like jewels. In recent backrooms history, the level's appearance and properties have been compared to those of [[Level 8]] and [[Level 39]]. Part of the level is dominated by long passageways which go in numerous directions. Each passageway contains several doors or holes, which lead to enclosed salt mines. Each of these mines consists either of a wide open mining area or a downward tunnel. {{Blurimage|file=coalandsalt.jpg|caption=An image of a mine in Level 620}} The hardness of salt crystals varies throughout the level. Crystals near the top of cave mines tend to be harder to break and more of a threat to wanderers. Soft crystals are more commonly found scattered all over the ground. Stepping on this type of crystal will make a soft crunching sound, similar to that of fresh snow. On the other hand, making contact with any hard crystal will strangely produce a loud creaking noise, similar to a rusty door . Noises inside the level are obnoxiously loud; all areas, including the salt mines, can reach 95 decibels, which posts a considerable hazard to wanderers. Screaming and shouting can commonly be heard from the interior of the mine. It was originally thought that this strange noise came from trapped wanderers, but it was later discovered that it was actually generated by the sound of constant mining. It has been recommended that wanderers bring earplugs to mitigate the noise issue. Despite the fact that the level initially appears only as a claustrophobic tunnel, walking down the passageway will lead to a collection of enormous mine areas. Each of these areas is more or less the same, containing an open space full of salt rocks with a pool of water below. Salt rocks are constantly mined by a robotic mining drill, which spits out salt rock into a moving container at varied rates. The container makes several trips around many mines, constantly collecting salt. After it is done collecting minerals, it disappears to an unknown location. Although some have speculated that it eventually noclips to a separate level, this has not been proven. Trying to follow the container poses a significant hazard to wanderers. If one gets too close, they may unwillingly noclip themselves. Safety is an aspect of the level that most wanderers fail to consider. Rock-hard salt has been found to fall off the top of caves and hit wanderers straight in the head. Although these falling rocks are comparable to [[Level 8]]'s stalagmites, it is important to note that once contact is made with the ground, the rocks instantly break into different pieces and launch over the surrounding area. In addition, the relatively diminutive amount of oxygen in the salt mine is usually noticed quickly by incoming wanderers. Canisters of oxygen and protection suits can be found scattered around the area, but usually only near large deposits of salt. Due to the uniform nature of the level, navigation is considerably difficult to manage. Navigation and communication devices, even high quality ones possessed by outposts, seem to lose battery quicker and regularly fail to function. In addition, no map has been sketched of the level since its discovery, leading members of [[M.E.G]] and other outposts to caution wanderers to not venture too far into the level. Small crevices can commonly be spotted near the top of cave mines. Although many of these holes may appear to be impassable, further examination proves otherwise. Most wanderers have found that despite the hardness of the rocks, they may be stretched, allowing for them to pass through. Going through these tiny entrances will inevitably lead to other floors. Higher floors are, for the most part, the same as the first floor. They are considered to be a more "extreme" version of the level, containing more suspect mines and entities. Currently, the number of total floors is unknown, but there are presumed to be at least five. {{Blurquote|image=thesaltmine.png|quote-text=The rocks won't stop falling|person=Jackson S. (Wanderer)}} ===Vegetation Event === Level 620 is infamously known for an annual vegetation event. During a time period of just one day, plants will suddenly take root and grow in almost every mine despite the conditions of the level. The level's properties may entirely change, including oxygen levels, which become abundant. By the end of the event, the entire level is usually entirely covered in various plants, resembling an entirely different place. It is currently unknown how the plants thrive in the environment or even get in the level in the first place. Although the origins of the vegetable growth were not thought about for a long time, there have been recent theories that have gained traction among wanderers who have stayed in the level. The most popular theory maintains that a certain crystal causes the extreme vegetation growth. A less popular idea states that there are secretly seeds planted in the ground underneath the salt rocks. Both theories still lack sufficient evidence to be declared factual.
Entities
[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] appear in wild numbers in this level, often appearing at intersections between several mines. Although both male and female moths can be found in Level 620, female ones can only been seen inside mines, while male ones can also be seen in hallways. Despite their crowded appearance, Deathmoths struggle to survive in the harsh conditions of the level. In certain areas of the level, thousands of dead moths can be found scattered on the floor, killed by the constant falling of rocks. Even with the present conditions, [[Movable Statues]] have been seen inside Level 620's hallways. This entity is known in the level for adhering to strange behavior. Although some statues are docile, others may try to mess with the wanderer in several ways, including blocking off the exit to mines for long periods of time. As per their usual properties, attacking them will cause them to become immediately hostile. Movable Statues have also been known to pull off other devious stunts. In several instances, wanderers have reported missing supplies; these incidents were quickly linked to the statues. Currently, there is little wanderers can do to combat this besides avoiding the entity.
Colonies and Outposts
Although [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]] originally set up a colony in the level when they discovered Level 620, it has since been abandoned. It was speculated that they intended to bring the level under their control, but decided that the level was of no particular use. A few years after, [[Backroom Colonists|The Backroom Colonists]] set up a research outpost in the level which was intended to "thrive for 50 years." This particular outpost was abandoned in 2022. Currently, there are only two active outposts that can be discovered in Level 620. ===The Salt Traders=== *This outpost consists of 40 members. *Their location can be in any mine. *They possess a large number of oxygen canisters. *Their main objective is to collect salt and sell it on other levels for a massive profit. * They are not accepting members and can be hostile to wanderers in certain cases. ===Jewel Research=== *This outpost consists of 25 members. *They can be found in any mine. *They possess a large number of oxygen canisters. * Unlike The Salt Traders, the main objective of Jewel Research is to collect and study the minerals in Level 620. *They are friendly and accepting members who have not been affiliated with The Salt Traders.
Entrances and Exits
Entrances
*Finding large chunks of salt rocks in barrels in [[Level 323]] will bring you here. *Finding cave-related documents in [[Level 462]] will bring you here. *Entering any salt cave at the base of The Cursed Mountains in [[Level 626.2]] may lead here.
Exits
*Making it deep enough in the mine may cause you to find yourself in [[Level 8]]. *Noclipping during The Vegetation Event will bring you to [[Level 110]]. *Following the moving container may bring you to [[Level 216]]. *Entering a mineshaft may take you to [[Level 258]]. *Tripping and falling in any mines may take you to a [[Level List|random level]]. *Falling down several mines at a time will cause you to enter [[The Void]].
